title: Karpatiosorbus pelsoensis
taxonomy: Karpatiosorbus pelsoensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, within the genus Karpatiosorbus. It is a hybrid species derived from the cross between Sorbus aucuparia and Sorbus torminalis. The classification of Karpatiosorbus pelsoensis as a hybrid species reflects its unique genetic makeup, which combines characteristics from its two parent species. This classification is based on molecular studies that have confirmed the plant's hybrid origin. As a result, Karpatiosorbus pelsoensis is often classified as a distinct taxon within the broader Rosaceae family.

intro: Karpatiosorbus pelsoensis, also known as Hungarian mountain ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Eastern Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and digestive issues. Its bioactive constituents, including polyphenolic compounds, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, are responsible for its medicinal properties. Karpatiosorbus pelsoensis is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
